source_boundary.notes: Rows are arXiv metadata receipts and requirements pressure only. They are not treated as proof of external robotics, medical, safety, or foundation-model claims.

vision language action models robotics benchmark
world models robotics embodied AI control
surgical robotics foundation model autonomous surgery

out_of_scope_examples.title: Robust Tabular Foundation Models
out_of_scope_examples.reason: Returned by broad foundation-model search but not robotics-specific enough for this packet.

workbench_requirements: Every sim-to-real claim declares robot morphology, actuator units, sensor units, environment geometry, obstacle state, command log, predicted trace, observed trace, latency, tolerances, and negative controls.
Vision-language-action and foundation-model claims require benchmark cards, task suites, hidden-test hygiene, action budgets, contamination checks, and real-robot safety boundaries.
Surgical, medical, and rehabilitation robotics claims require clinical non-claim language unless primary clinical evidence, approvals, and domain review receipts exist.
Soft-robotics and biology-inspired claims require material model assumptions, actuation physics, environment coupling, and measured deformation traces before promotion.
BuildLang/buildc should carry typed units, kinematics/dynamics kernels, trace schemas, safety envelopes, and verifier receipts after the JavaScript fixture stabilizes.
